title: The Forgetting Problem
concept: Catastrophic forgetting occurs when a neural network is trained on new data and overwrites the weights that stored its previously learned knowledge.

exercises.prompt: How does a neural network store the patterns it learns?
exercises.explain: When a neural network learns, it adjusts its internal connections, which are called weights.

cards.title: AI Memory Loss
cards.body: Have you ever studied so hard for a math test that you completely forgot how to speak Spanish? AI models do this all the time! When they learn something new, they often wipe their memory of the old things they knew.

cards.title: Catastrophic Forgetting
cards.body: Catastrophic forgetting is when an AI model is trained on a new task and completely erases its ability to do a previous task. Because its internal connections (weights) change to learn the new skill, the old skill is overwritten and lost.

cards.title: The Eraser Analogy
cards.body: Imagine a digital diary with only one page. If you write your school notes on it, they are safe. But tomorrow, when you write your shopping list on that same page, you have to erase your school notes first. The old information is gone forever because you wrote over it!

cards.title: From Cats to Cars
cards.body: Imagine training an AI to recognize photos of cats. It gets really good at it! But then, you train that same AI to recognize cars instead. If you show it a cat photo now, it will fail completely. The new 'car' training wiped out its 'cat' knowledge.

cards.title: Not Like a Hard Drive
cards.body: People think AI brains are like computer hard drives that can store everything in separate folders. In reality, neural networks share the same space for all tasks, so new lessons can easily overwrite old ones if we aren't careful.

cards.title: Human vs. AI Learning
cards.body: Standard neural networks don't have separate folders for different skills. They share the same network structure, which leads to memory overwrites.

right.text: Erases the old skill completely to free up space for the new skill.

left.text: Keeps old skills (like riding a bike) safe while learning new ones (like cooking).

exercises.prompt: What happens if you train a standard neural network on Task A, and then train it on Task B?

options.text: It gets twice as good at the first task.
It completely forgets how to do the first task.
It merges the two tasks into a brand new language.
The AI system shuts down and crashes.

exercises.explain: When a single neural network is trained on a second task without special protection, it overwrites the settings (weights) it used for the first task, resulting in catastrophic forgetting.

exercises.prompt: True or False: Catastrophic forgetting happens because a neural network uses the same weights to learn different tasks.
exercises.explain: This is true. Because neural networks share the same connections (weights) for everything they do, learning new info without safeguards will overwrite old info.

exercises.prompt: Fill in the missing word to complete the explanation of forgetting.
exercises.sentence: When an AI learns a new task, it changes its internal weights and overwrites its old knowledge.
exercises.explain: A neural network stores what it learns in its weights. Changing these weights to learn a new task is what causes it to forget the old task.

exercises.prompt: Which of these systems is showing the classic behavior of Catastrophic Forgetting?

options.text: System A: Learns to play chess, then learns to paint, but can no longer play chess at all.
System B: Learns to ride a bike, then learns to bake, and can still do both easily.
System C: Learns to read books and improves its speech at the exact same time.

exercises.explain: System A is the AI because it suffers from catastrophic forgetting (losing old skills when learning new ones), which is a major challenge in artificial neural networks. Humans (System B) can learn to bake without forgetting how to ride a bike.

exercises.prompt: Why does a neural network suffer from catastrophic forgetting when trained on a new task?

options.text: The network's memory card gets physically unplugged.
The network runs out of battery power and shuts down.
Updating weights for the new task overwrites the weights used for the old task.
The old training data was corrupt and broke the system code.

exercises.explain: Catastrophic forgetting happens when a neural network updates its internal weights to learn a new task, completely overwriting the weights it used to solve the old task. This happens because the model has no built-in way to lock or protect important old knowledge.
